zotero.abstractNote: The mammalian neocortex is organized into layers, but the functional significance of this laminar architecture remains poorly understood. We performed a dense connectomic reconstruction of a 100-micrometer-cubed volume of layer 4 in mouse primary somatosensory cortex. We identified all neurons and their synaptic connections, revealing that the local circuit is dominated by a dense, non-random network of excitatory neurons. This network is organized into a modular structure that is consistent with the functional organization of the cortex. Our results provide a structural basis for understanding the computational role of layer 4 in sensory processing.
